# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.

Prevent hardcoded secrets.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# AWS Deployment Script
# Supports both local development and CI/CD environments
# =============================================================================

# ---- Configuration (override via environment variables) ----
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}"
APP_NAME="${APP_NAME:-myapp}"
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-staging}"
S3_BUCKET="${S3_BUCKET:-}"
ECR_REPOSITORY="${ECR_REPOSITORY:-}"
ECS_CLUSTER="${ECS_CLUSTER:-}"
ECS_SERVICE="${ECS_SERVICE:-}"
LAMBDA_FUNCTION="${LAMBDA_FUNCTION:-}"
DEPLOY_TARGET="${DEPLOY_TARGET:-s3}"  # s3, ecs, lambda
DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG="${DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/aws-deploy-${APP_NAME}-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"

# ---- Color output helpers ----
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" >&2
}

log_step() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

# ---- Cleanup on exit ----
cleanup() {
    local exit_code=$?
    if [[ $exit_code -ne 0 ]]; then
        log_error "Deployment failed with exit code $exit_code. Check log: $LOG_FILE"
    fi
    # Remove any temporary files
    rm -f /tmp/aws-deploy-task-def-*.json 2>/dev/null || true
    exit $exit_code
}

trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'log_error "Script interrupted"; exit 130' INT TERM

# ---- Prerequisite checks ----
check_prerequisites() {
    log_step "Checking prerequisites..."

    local missing=()

    if ! command -v aws &>/dev/null; then
        missing+=("aws-cli")
    fi

    if [[ "$DEPLOY_TARGET" == "ecs" ]]; then
        if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("docker")
        fi
        if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("jq")
        fi
    fi

    if [[ "$DEPLOY_TARGET" == "lambda" ]]; then
        if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("jq")
        fi
        if ! command -v zip &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("zip")
        fi
    fi

    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        log_error "Missing required tools: ${missing[*]}"
        exit 1
    fi

    log_info "All prerequisites met."
}

# ---- AWS Authentication ----
authenticate_aws() {
    log_step "Authenticating with AWS..."

    # Method 1: CI/CD environment variables (e.g., GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins)
    # Expects AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY set as CI/CD secrets
    if [[ -n "${CI:-}" || -n "${GITHUB_ACTIONS:-}" || -n "${GITLAB_CI:-}" || -n "${JENKINS_URL:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "CI/CD environment detected."

        if [[ -z "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-}" || -z "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-}" ]]; then
            # Check for IAM role (e.g., EC2 instance role, ECS task role, GitHub OIDC)
            if aws sts get-caller-identity &>/dev/null; then
                log_info "Authenticated via IAM role / instance profile."
            else
                log_error "AWS credentials not found. Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY as CI/CD secrets, or attach an IAM role."
                exit 1
            fi
        else
            log_info "Using AWS credentials from environment variables."
        fi

    # Method 2: AWS SSO / Named profile for local development
    elif [[ -n "${AWS_PROFILE:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Using AWS profile: $AWS_PROFILE"

        # Check if SSO session is valid; if not, initiate login
        if ! aws sts get-caller-identity --profile "$AWS_PROFILE" &>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "AWS SSO session expired or invalid. Initiating login..."
            aws sso login --profile "$AWS_PROFILE"
        fi

    # Method 3: AWS Vault (for local development with MFA)
    elif command -v aws-vault &>/dev/null && [[ -n "${AWS_VAULT_PROFILE:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Using aws-vault with profile: $AWS_VAULT_PROFILE"
        # aws-vault should already have injected credentials into the environment
        if ! aws sts get-caller-identity &>/dev/null; then
            log_error "aws-vault credentials not valid. Run: aws-vault exec $AWS_VAULT_PROFILE -- $0"
            exit 1
        fi

    # Method 4: Default credential chain (shared credentials file, instance profile, etc.)
    else
        log_info "Using default AWS credential chain."
        if ! aws sts get-caller-identity &>/dev/null; then
            log_error "No valid AWS credentials found. Configure credentials using one of:"
            log_error "  - AWS_PROFILE environment variable (for SSO/named profiles)"
            log_error "  - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (for CI/CD)"
            log_error "  - IAM instance role"
            log_error "  - aws configure"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Assume a deployment role if specified
    if [[ -n "${AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Assuming deployment role: $AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN"
        local session_name="deploy-${APP_NAME}-$(date +%s)"
        local assume_output
        assume_output=$(aws sts assume-role \
            --role-arn "$AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN" \
            --role-session-name "$session_name" \
            --duration-seconds 3600 \
            --output json)

        export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
        export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
        export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN
        AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$(echo "$assume_output" | jq -r '.Credentials.AccessKeyId')
        AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$(echo "$assume_output" | jq -r '.Credentials.SecretAccessKey')
        AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=$(echo "$assume_output" | jq -r '.Credentials.SessionToken')

        log_info "Successfully assumed role."
    fi

    # Verify and display identity (mask account details in logs)
    local caller_identity
    caller_identity=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --output json --region "$AWS_REGION")
    local account_id
    account_id=$(echo "$caller_identity" | jq -r '.Account')
    local arn
    arn=$(echo "$caller_identity" | jq -r '.Arn')

    log_info "Authenticated as: $arn (Account: ${account_id:0:4}****${account_id: -2})"
    log_info "Region: $AWS_REGION"
}

# ---- Deployment: S3 (Static site / artifacts) ----
deploy_s3() {
    log_step "Deploying to S3..."

    if [[ -z "$S3_BUCKET" ]]; then
        log_error "S3_BUCKET is not set."
        exit 1
    fi

    local source_dir="${SOURCE_DIR:-./dist}"

    if [[ ! -d "$source_dir" ]]; then
        log_error "Source directory '$source_dir' does not exist."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Verify bucket exists
    if ! aws s3api head-bucket --bucket "$S3_BUCKET" --region "$AWS_REGION" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "S3 bucket '$S3_BUCKET' does not exist or is not accessible."
        exit 1
    fi

    log_info "Syncing '$source_dir' to s3://$S3_BUCKET/"

    aws s3 sync "$source_dir" "s3://$S3_BUCKET/" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --delete \
        --exclude ".git/*" \
        --exclude ".env*" \
        --exclude "*.log" \
        | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

    # Invalidate CloudFront cache if distribution ID is provided
    if [[ -n "${CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Invalidating CloudFront distribution: $CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID"
        aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "$CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID" \
            --paths "/*" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    fi

    log_info "S3 deployment complete."
}

# ---- Deployment: ECS (Docker containers) ----
deploy_ecs() {
    log_step "Deploying to ECS..."

    if [[ -z "$ECR_REPOSITORY" || -z "$ECS_CLUSTER" || -z "$ECS_SERVICE" ]]; then
        log_error "ECR_REPOSITORY, ECS_CLUSTER, and ECS_SERVICE must all be set."
        exit 1
    fi

    local account_id
    account_id=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Account' --output text --region "$AWS_REGION")
    local ecr_uri="${account_id}.dkr.ecr.${AWS_REGION}.amazonaws.com/${ECR_REPOSITORY}"
    local image_uri="${ecr_uri}:${DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG}"

    # Authenticate Docker with ECR
    log_info "Authenticating Docker with ECR..."
    aws ecr get-login-password --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin "${account_id}.dkr.ecr.${AWS_REGION}.amazonaws.com"

    # Build Docker image
    local dockerfile="${DOCKERFILE:-Dockerfile}"
    local build_context="${BUILD_CONTEXT:-.}"

    log_info "Building Docker image: $image_uri"
    docker build \
        -t "$image_uri" \
        -f "$dockerfile" \
        --build-arg APP_ENV="$ENVIRONMENT" \
        "$build_context" \
        2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

    # Push to ECR
    log_info "Pushing image to ECR..."
    docker push "$image_uri" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

    # Update ECS service with new image
    log_info "Fetching current task definition..."
    local task_def_arn
    task_def_arn=$(aws ecs describe-services \
        --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
        --services "$ECS_SERVICE" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'services[0].taskDefinition' \
        --output text)

    local task_def
    task_def=$(aws ecs describe-task-definition \
        --task-definition "$task_def_arn" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'taskDefinition' \
        --output json)

    # Update the image in the task definition
    local new_task_def
    new_task_def=$(echo "$task_def" | jq \
        --arg IMAGE "$image_uri" \
        '.containerDefinitions[0].image = $IMAGE |
         del(.taskDefinitionArn, .revision, .status, .requiresAttributes, .compatibilities, .registeredAt, .registeredBy)')

    local tmp_task_def="/tmp/aws-deploy-task-def-$$.json"
    echo "$new_task_def" > "$tmp_task_def"

    log_info "Registering new task definition..."
    local new_task_def_arn
    new_task_def_arn=$(aws ecs register-task-definition \
        --cli-input-json "file://$tmp_task_def" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'taskDefinition.taskDefinitionArn' \
        --output text)

    rm -f "$tmp_task_def"

    log_info "Updating ECS service with new task definition..."
    aws ecs update-service \
        --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
        --service "$ECS_SERVICE" \
        --task-definition "$new_task_def_arn" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

    # Wait for deployment to stabilize
    if [[ "${WAIT_FOR_STABLE:-true}" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Waiting for ECS service to stabilize (timeout: 10 minutes)..."
        if aws ecs wait services-stable \
            --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
            --services "$ECS_SERVICE" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log_info "ECS service is stable."
        else
            log_error "ECS service did not stabilize within the timeout period."
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    log_info "ECS deployment complete. Image: $image_uri"
}

# ---- Deployment: Lambda ----
deploy_lambda() {
    log_step "Deploying to Lambda..."

    if [[ -z "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" ]]; then
        log_error "LAMBDA_FUNCTION is not set."
        exit 1
    fi

    local source_dir="${SOURCE_DIR:-.}"
    local zip_file="/tmp/aws-deploy-lambda-${APP_NAME}-$$.zip"

    # Check if deploying a container image or zip package
    if [[ -n "${LAMBDA_IMAGE_URI:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Updating Lambda function with container image: $LAMBDA_IMAGE_URI"
        aws lambda update-function-code \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
            --image-uri "$LAMBDA_IMAGE_URI" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    else
        # Package as zip
        log_info "Packaging Lambda function from '$source_dir'..."
        (cd "$source_dir" && zip -r "$zip_file" . \
            -x ".git/*" \
            -x ".env*" \
            -x "*.log" \
            -x "node_modules/.cache/*" \
            -x "__pycache__/*" \
            -x "*.pyc") 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

        local zip_size
        zip_size=$(du -h "$zip_file" | cut -f1)
        log_info "Package size: $zip_size"

        log_info "Updating Lambda function code..."
        aws lambda update-function-code \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
            --zip-file "fileb://$zip_file" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

        rm -f "$zip_file"
    fi

    # Wait for function to be updated
    log_info "Waiting for Lambda function to be updated..."
    aws lambda wait function-updated-v2 \
        --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION"

    # Update environment variables if provided
    if [[ -n "${LAMBDA_ENV_VARS:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "Updating Lambda environment variables..."
        aws lambda update-function-configuration \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
            --environment "Variables={$LAMBDA_ENV_VARS}" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

        aws lambda wait function-updated-v2 \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION"
    fi

    # Publish a new version if requested
    if [[ "${LAMBDA_PUBLISH:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Publishing new Lambda version..."
        local version
        version=$(aws lambda publish-version \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            --query 'Version' \
            --output text)
        log_info "Published Lambda version: $version"

        # Update alias if specified
        if [[ -n "${LAMBDA_ALIAS:-}" ]]; then
            log_info "Updating alias '$LAMBDA_ALIAS' to version $version..."
            aws lambda update-alias \
                --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
                --name "$LAMBDA_ALIAS" \
                --function-version "$version" \
                --region "$AWS_REGION" \
                --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Lambda deployment complete."
}

# ---- Health check ----
run_health_check() {
    if [[ -z "${HEALTH_CHECK_URL:-}" ]]; then
        log_info "No HEALTH_CHECK_URL set, skipping health check."
        return 0
    fi

    log_step "Running health check: $HEALTH_CHECK_URL"

    local max_retries="${HEALTH_CHECK_RETRIES:-10}"
    local retry_interval="${HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL:-15}"
    local attempt=1

    while [[ $attempt -le $max_retries ]]; do
        local http_code
        http_code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 10 "$HEALTH_CHECK_URL" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")

        if [[ "$http_code" =~ ^2[0-9]{2}$ ]]; then
            log_info "Health check passed (HTTP $http_code) on attempt $attempt."
            return 0
        fi

        log_warn "Health check attempt $attempt/$max_retries failed (HTTP $http_code). Retrying in ${retry_interval}s..."
        sleep "$retry_interval"
        ((attempt++))
    done

    log_error "Health check failed after $max_retries attempts."
    return 1
}

# ---- Rollback ----
rollback() {
    log_step "Initiating rollback..."

    case "$DEPLOY_TARGET" in
        ecs)
            if [[ -n "${PREVIOUS_TASK_DEF_ARN:-}" ]]; then
                log_info "Rolling back ECS service to previous task definition: $PREVIOUS_TASK_DEF_ARN"
                aws ecs update-service \
                    --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
                    --service "$ECS_SERVICE" \
                    --task-definition "$PREVIOUS_TASK_DEF_ARN" \
                    --region "$AWS_REGION" \
                    --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
            else
                log_warn "No previous task definition ARN available for rollback."
            fi
            ;;
        lambda)
            if [[ -n "${LAMBDA_ALIAS:-}" && -n "${PREVIOUS_LAMBDA_VERSION:-}" ]]; then
                log_info "Rolling back Lambda alias '$LAMBDA_ALIAS' to version $PREVIOUS_LAMBDA_VERSION"
                aws lambda update-alias \
                    --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION" \
                    --name "$LAMBDA_ALIAS" \
                    --function-version "$PREVIOUS_LAMBDA_VERSION" \
                    --region "$AWS_REGION" \
                    --output json | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
            else
                log_warn "No previous Lambda version available for rollback."
            fi
            ;;
        s3)
            log_warn "S3 rollback requires versioned bucket. Manual intervention may be needed."
            ;;
        *)
            log_warn "Rollback not supported for deploy target: $DEPLOY_TARGET"
            ;;
    esac
}

# ---- Usage ----
usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS]

AWS Deployment Script - Deploy applications to AWS services.

Options:
    -t, --target TARGET     Deployment target: s3, ecs, lambda (default: s3)
    -e, --environment ENV   Deployment environment (default: staging)
    -r, --region REGION     AWS region (default: us-east-1)
    -n, --app-name NAME     Application name (default: myapp)
    --dry-run               Show what would be done without executing
    --rollback              Perform a rollback instead of deployment
    -h, --help              Show this help message

Environment Variables:
    AWS_PROFILE             AWS CLI profile name (local development)
    AWS_REGION              AWS region
    AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN     IAM role ARN to assume for deployment
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID       AWS access key (CI/CD - set as secret)
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY   AWS secret key (CI/CD - set as secret)

    S3_BUCKET               S3 bucket name (for s3 target)
    CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID  CloudFront distribution to invalidate
    SOURCE_DIR              Source directory to deploy (default: ./dist or .)

    ECR_REPOSITORY          ECR repository name (for ecs target)
    ECS_CLUSTER             ECS cluster name
    ECS_SERVICE             ECS service name
    DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG        Docker image tag (default: latest)
    DOCKERFILE              Dockerfile path (default: Dockerfile)
    BUILD_CONTEXT           Docker build context (default: .)
    WAIT_FOR_STABLE         Wait for ECS service stability (default: true)

    LAMBDA_FUNCTION         Lambda function name (for lambda target)
    LAMBDA_IMAGE_URI        Lambda container image URI
    LAMBDA_PUBLISH          Publish new version (default: false)
    LAMBDA_ALIAS            Lambda alias to update
    LAMBDA_ENV_VARS         Comma-separated KEY=VALUE pairs

    HEALTH_CHECK_URL        URL for post-deployment health check
    HEALTH_CHECK_RETRIES    Number of health check retries (default: 10)
    HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL   Seconds between retries (default: 15)

Examples:
    # Deploy static site to S3 (local dev with SSO)
    AWS_PROFILE=myprofile S3_BUCKET=my-website ./$(basename "$0") -t s3

    # Deploy to ECS (CI/CD)
    ECR_REPOSITORY=myapp ECS_CLUSTER=prod ECS_SERVICE=myapp-svc \\
        ./$(basename "$0") -t ecs -e production

    # Deploy Lambda function
    LAMBDA_FUNCTION=my-function LAMBDA_PUBLISH=true LAMBDA_ALIAS=live \\
        ./$(basename "$0") -t lambda
EOF
}

# ---- Main ----
main() {
    local dry_run=false
    local do_rollback=false

    # Parse arguments
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -t|--target)
                DEPLOY_TARGET="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -e|--environment)
                ENVIRONMENT="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -r|--region)
                AWS_REGION="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -n|--app-name)
                APP_NAME="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --dry-run)
                dry_run=true
                shift
                ;;
            --rollback)
                do_rollback=true
                shift
                ;;
            -h|--help)
                usage
                exit 0
                ;;
            *)
                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
                usage
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
    done

    echo ""
    log_info "========================================="
    log_info "  AWS Deployment Script"
    log_info "  App:         $APP_NAME"
    log_info "  Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
    log_info "  Target:      $DEPLOY_TARGET"
    log_info "  Region:      $AWS_REGION"
    log_info "  Log:         $LOG_FILE"
    log_info "========================================="
    echo ""

    # Validate deploy target
    case "$DEPLOY_TARGET" in
        s3|ecs|lambda) ;;
        *)
            log_error "Invalid deploy target: $DEPLOY_TARGET. Must be one of: s3, ecs, lambda"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Check prerequisites
    check_prerequisites

    # Authenticate
    authenticate_aws

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "DRY RUN mode - no changes will be made."
        log_info "Would deploy '$APP_NAME' to '$DEPLOY_TARGET' in '$ENVIRONMENT' environment."
        exit 0
    fi

    # Rollback mode
    if [[ "$do_rollback" == "true" ]]; then
        rollback
        exit $?
    fi

    # Store previous state for potential rollback
    if [[ "$DEPLOY_TARGET" == "ecs" && -n "$ECS_CLUSTER" && -n "$ECS_SERVICE" ]]; then
        PREVIOUS_TASK_DEF_ARN=$(aws ecs describe-services \
            --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
            --services "$ECS_SERVICE" \
            --region "$AWS_REGION" \
            --query 'services[0].taskDefinition' \
            --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        export PREVIOUS_TASK_DEF_ARN
    fi

    # Deploy
    local deploy_start
    deploy_start=$(date +%s)

    case "$DEPLOY_TARGET" in
        s3)
            deploy_s3
            ;;
        ecs)
            deploy_ecs
            ;;
        lambda)
            deploy_lambda
            ;;
    esac

    # Health check
    if ! run_health_check; then
        log_error "Health check failed. Attempting rollback..."
        rollback
        exit 1
    fi

    local deploy_end
    deploy_end=$(date +%s)
    local duration=$((deploy_end - deploy_start))

    echo ""
    log_info "========================================="
    log_info "  Deployment Successful!"
    log_info "  Duration: ${duration}s"
    log_info "  Target:   $DEPLOY_TARGET"
    log_info "  Env:      $ENVIRONMENT"
    log_info "========================================="
    echo ""
}

main "$@"
